title: What is the intended/recommended way to view verilator dot files graphically
body:
Hi, I've been fiddling with verilator, dot and ghostscript to view fotfiles outputted with `verilator --debug`. But my end result is always so bad resolution that it is unreadable. This is how I've done it:

```
verilator --cc --debug
dot -Tps2 -O ./obj_dir/Vverilator_048_gate_opt.dot
ps2pdf -dDEVICEWIDTHPOINTS=27389 -dDEVICEHEIGHTPOINTS=1903 -dEPSCrop ./obj_dir/Vverilator_048_gate_opt.dot.ps2
firefox Vverilator_048_gate_opt.dot.ps2.pdf
```

At some point I needed specific DEVICEWIDTHPOINTS, otherwise The generated PDF was just standard format and I could only view 1 % of my graph (it looked like the top left corner). But maybe that was when I used `dot -Tps` because with `dot -Tps2` it doesn't seem to make a difference.

When I view it with firefox, I can zoom in, but at 100 % zoom, everything is extremely blurry (both text, circles and lines).

I also tried look at the generated ps file with ghostscript (once again needing -dDEVICEWIDTHPOINTS etc to not crop the image of the graph).
`ghostscript -dDEVICEWIDTHPOINTS=27389 -dDEVICEHEIGHTPOINTS=1903 ./obj_dir/Vverilator_048_gate_opt.dot.ps2`
This starts the ghostscript application with a really small windows, without any menu bar and I have no idea how to zoom or do anything except looking at this miniature. (I have never used ghostscript before so maybe there is some way to look at a portion of the graph with this tool)

How can I look at my graph with reasonable resolution? I'm sure there is some better way to do this that I haven't figured out. All help appreciated. :)
